# HoheiselIT Lexoffice Laravel

> Laravel package that synchronizes Laravel models with the Lexoffice accounting API. Supports contacts, invoices, and products via event-driven queue jobs with automatic retry, sync logging, and incoming webhook handling.

## Installation

```bash
composer require hoheiselIT/lexoffice-laravel
php artisan vendor:publish --tag=lexoffice-config
php artisan migrate
```

Add to `.env`:

```env
LEXOFFICE_API_KEY=your-api-key
LEXOFFICE_QUEUE=lexoffice
LEXOFFICE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=your-webhook-secret
LEXOFFICE_WEBHOOK_PATH=lexoffice/webhook
```

## Core Concepts

The package provides three layers:

1. **Direct API access** via `LexofficeClient` (or `Lexoffice` facade) for raw Lexoffice API calls.
2. **Model sync** via `HasLexofficeSync` trait + Contract interfaces — model saves automatically dispatch queue jobs.
3. **Webhook handling** — incoming Lexoffice webhooks are verified, parsed, and dispatched as typed Laravel events.

## Syncing Models

Implement the correct contract on your Eloquent model and add the `HasLexofficeSync` trait. The trait hooks into the Eloquent `saved` event and dispatches a queue job automatically.

### Contacts

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Contracts\SyncableContact;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Traits\HasLexofficeSync;

class Customer extends Model implements SyncableContact
{
    use HasLexofficeSync;

    public function toLexofficeContact(): array
    {
        return [
            'roles' => ['customer' => []],
            'company' => ['name' => $this->company_name],
            'emailAddresses' => ['business' => [$this->email]],
        ];
    }

    public function getLexofficeId(): ?string
    {
        return $this->lexoffice_id;
    }

    public function setLexofficeId(string $id): void
    {
        $this->update(['lexoffice_id' => $id]);
    }
}
```

### Invoices

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Contracts\SyncableInvoice;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Traits\HasLexofficeSync;

class Order extends Model implements SyncableInvoice
{
    use HasLexofficeSync;

    public function toLexofficeInvoice(): array
    {
        return [
            'voucherDate' => $this->created_at->toIso8601String(),
            'address' => [
                'name' => $this->customer->company_name,
                'contactId' => $this->customer->lexoffice_id,
            ],
            'lineItems' => $this->items->map(fn ($item) => [
                'type' => 'custom',
                'name' => $item->name,
                'quantity' => $item->quantity,
                'unitName' => 'Stück',
                'unitPrice' => [
                    'currency' => 'EUR',
                    'netAmount' => $item->price_net,
                    'taxRatePercentage' => 19,
                ],
            ])->toArray(),
            'taxConditions' => ['taxType' => 'net'],
            'totalPrice' => ['currency' => 'EUR'],
        ];
    }

    public function getLexofficeId(): ?string
    {
        return $this->lexoffice_id;
    }

    public function setLexofficeId(string $id): void
    {
        $this->update(['lexoffice_id' => $id]);
    }
}
```

### Products

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Contracts\SyncableProduct;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Traits\HasLexofficeSync;

class Product extends Model implements SyncableProduct
{
    use HasLexofficeSync;

    public function toLexofficeProduct(): array
    {
        return [
            'title' => $this->name,
            'description' => $this->description,
            'type' => 'PRODUCT',
            'unitName' => 'Stück',
            'price' => [
                'netPrice' => $this->price,
                'currency' => 'EUR',
                'leadingPrice' => 'NET',
            ],
        ];
    }

    public function getLexofficeId(): ?string
    {
        return $this->lexoffice_id;
    }

    public function setLexofficeId(string $id): void
    {
        $this->update(['lexoffice_id' => $id]);
    }
}
```

## Manual Sync

```php
// Sync immediately (synchronous, bypasses queue):
$customer->syncToLexoffice();

// Dispatch to queue manually:
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Jobs\SyncContactJob;
SyncContactJob::dispatch($customer);
```

## Artisan Command

```bash
# Sync all models of a class:
php artisan lexoffice:sync "App\Models\Customer"

# Sync specific IDs only:
php artisan lexoffice:sync "App\Models\Customer" --ids=1 --ids=2
```

## Direct API Access (Facade)

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Facades\Lexoffice;

// Contacts
$contact  = Lexoffice::contacts->find('uuid');
$contacts = Lexoffice::contacts->search(email: 'max@example.com');
$created  = Lexoffice::contacts->create([...]);
$updated  = Lexoffice::contacts->update('uuid', [...]);

// Invoices
$invoice  = Lexoffice::invoices->create([...]);
$invoice  = Lexoffice::invoices->finalize($invoice['id']);
$document = Lexoffice::invoices->renderDocument($invoice['id']);

// Voucher list
$list = Lexoffice::vouchers->list();

// Webhook subscriptions
Lexoffice::webhooks->subscribe('contact.changed', 'https://your-app.com/lexoffice/webhook');
Lexoffice::webhooks->list();
Lexoffice::webhooks->unsubscribe($subscriptionId);
```

## Webhooks

Incoming webhooks from Lexoffice are received at `POST /lexoffice/webhook`. The route is auto-registered. The `X-Lxo-Signature` header is verified via HMAC-SHA256 using `LEXOFFICE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

Listen for typed events in `EventServiceProvider`:

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\ContactChanged;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\ContactCreated;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\ContactDeleted;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\InvoiceChanged;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\InvoiceCreated;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\InvoiceDeleted;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\PaymentChanged;
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Events\Webhooks\VoucherChanged;

protected $listen = [
    ContactChanged::class => [YourContactSyncListener::class],
    InvoiceChanged::class => [YourInvoiceListener::class],
    PaymentChanged::class => [YourPaymentListener::class],
];
```

Each event has: `$event->eventType`, `$event->resourceId`, `$event->organizationId`, `$event->payload`.

Unknown event types fire `LexofficeWebhookReceived` as fallback.

## Sync Log

Every sync attempt is written to the `lexoffice_sync_log` database table.

```php
use HoheiselIT\Lexoffice\Models\LexofficeSyncLog;

// All failures:
LexofficeSyncLog::failed()->latest()->get();

// History for a model:
LexofficeSyncLog::forModel(Customer::class, $id)->get();

// Last successful sync:
LexofficeSyncLog::forModel(Customer::class, $id)->successful()->latest()->first();
```

Log columns: `model_type`, `model_id`, `lexoffice_id`, `sync_type` (contact/invoice/product), `status` (success/failed), `payload` (JSON), `error` (string).

## Events Fired by the Package

| Event | When |
|-------|------|
| `LexofficeSynced` | After every successful outbound sync (contact/invoice/product) |
| `LexofficeWebhookReceived` | After every incoming webhook (fallback for unknown types) |
| `ContactCreated/Changed/Deleted` | Incoming webhook for contacts |
| `InvoiceCreated/Changed/Deleted` | Incoming webhook for invoices |
| `PaymentChanged` | Incoming webhook for payment status changes |
| `VoucherChanged` | Incoming webhook for voucher changes |

## Configuration Reference

```php
// config/lexoffice.php
return [
    'api_key'  => env('LEXOFFICE_API_KEY'),
    'base_url' => env('LEXOFFICE_BASE_URL', 'https://api.lexoffice.io/v1'),

    'queue' => [
        'connection' => env('LEXOFFICE_QUEUE_CONNECTION', null),
        'name'       => env('LEXOFFICE_QUEUE', 'lexoffice'),
    ],

    'retry' => [
        'times'           => 3,
        'backoff_base'    => 5,  // exponential: 5s → 10s → 20s
        'rate_limit_base' => 30, // exponential on 429: 30s → 60s → 120s
    ],

    'sync' => [
        'contacts' => true,
        'invoices' => true,
        'products' => true,
    ],

    'webhook' => [
        'secret'     => env('LEXOFFICE_WEBHOOK_SECRET'),
        'path'       => env('LEXOFFICE_WEBHOOK_PATH', 'lexoffice/webhook'),
        'middleware' => ['api'],
    ],
];
```

## Exceptions

| Exception | When |
|-----------|------|
| `LexofficeAuthException` | API key invalid (HTTP 401) |
| `LexofficeRateLimitException` | Rate limit hit (HTTP 429) — job retries with exponential backoff: 30s → 60s → 120s |
| `LexofficeApiException` | Any other API error |

All exceptions extend `LexofficeApiException extends RuntimeException`.
